title: EXPRESSO — anti-PD-1 monotherapy in melanoma
deck: 240 melanoma patients across six published anti-PD-1 cohorts. The framework's structural call separates the responders the field tools cannot.
prediction_headline: Encounter predicts a 27pp gap between Activation-held and Past-Activation patients, where PD-L1 separates only ~14pp.
result_note: Leave-one-cohort-out CV: predicted held 22.2% (observed 21.4%, error 0.8pp); predicted past 49.4% (observed 48.2%, error 1.2pp).

title: STAR-221 — domvanalimab + zimberelimab + chemo in 1L gastric/GEJ
deck: Phase 3 testing whether a TIGIT inhibitor adds anything to PD-1 + chemo in upper GI cancers. Discontinued for futility December 2025 — confirming the framework's pre-publication call.
drug: Domvanalimab + zimberelimab + chemo vs nivolumab + chemo
prediction_headline: PFS HR 0.95–1.05 (flat); ORR Δ 0–3pp; no PD-L1 subgroup interaction.
result_observed: Discontinued for futility at interim OS analysis
result_delta: Domvanalimab combination did not improve OS vs nivolumab + chemo control
result_note: STAR-221 was discontinued December 12, 2025 following independent data monitoring committee review of prespecified interim OS analysis. Per Arcus: "Patients in the domvanalimab-containing arm derived the same benefit as patients treated in the control arm." Eighth Phase 3 TIGIT trial to fail across four cancer types. The framework's structural call (FAIL — TIGIT and PD-1 occupy the same step) was published in v1 on April 3, 2026, but the trial had already been terminated four months earlier; this is therefore a pre-publication confirmation analogous to KeyVibe-006 and ceralasertib.

title: VOLGA — durvalumab + tremelimumab + enfortumab vedotin (bladder periop)
deck: Phase 3 perioperative trial in cisplatin-ineligible muscle-invasive bladder cancer. Reads out April 2026.
prediction_headline: pCR triplet ≥50% (likely 55–65%), pCR control ~25–30%, Δ ≥20pp, ctDNA clearance ≥70%.

title: Harmony Melanoma — fianlimab + cemiplimab vs pembrolizumab in 1L melanoma
deck: Phase 3 head-to-head testing whether adding LAG-3 to PD-1 covers a second step in the immune cycle. Includes a cemiplimab monotherapy arm — the cleanest possible test of the LAG-3 attribution. Enrollment closed; reads H2 2026.
drug: Fianlimab + cemiplimab vs pembrolizumab vs cemiplimab monotherapy
prediction_headline: PFS HR 0.70–0.80; ORR experimental ~44% vs control ~34%; CR rate 20–25%; cemiplimab monotherapy arm clinically equivalent to pembrolizumab.

title: ENERGIZE — neoadjuvant chemo ± nivolumab ± linrodostat in cisplatin-eligible MIBC
deck: Phase 3 testing whether IDO1 inhibition adds anything to PD-1 + chemo in cisplatin-eligible perioperative bladder cancer. Three-arm design produces the cleanest test of the coverage principle in any trial currently on the scorecard. Enrollment complete; reads ASCO 2026.
prediction_headline: Arm B (gem/cis + nivo) > Arm A (gem/cis); Arm C (gem/cis + nivo + linrodostat) ≈ Arm B. Critical test = (B > A) AND (C ≈ B).

title: IMbrave251 — atezolizumab continuation with TKI in 2L HCC after atezo + bev
deck: Phase 3 testing whether continuing atezolizumab with a TKI helps patients who progressed on first-line atezolizumab + bevacizumab. CONTACT-03 already showed that ICI rechallenge after ICI failure does not generate a new result. Active, not recruiting.
drug: Atezolizumab + (lenvatinib or sorafenib) vs (lenvatinib or sorafenib) alone

title: KEYLYNK-012 — pembrolizumab + CRT ± olaparib vs CRT + durvalumab in Stage III NSCLC
deck: Phase 3 testing two questions at once: whether pembro + concurrent CRT followed by pembro beats CRT + durvalumab consolidation, and whether adding olaparib to the pembro consolidation arm helps in unselected NSCLC. KEYLYNK-008 (similar olaparib combination in metastatic squamous NSCLC) was already discontinued for futility April 2024. Active enrolling.
drug: Pembro + CRT → pembro ± olaparib vs CRT → durvalumab
prediction_headline: No PFS or OS benefit from adding olaparib to pembro consolidation in unselected population. Possible signal in HRD-positive subgroup if reported.

title: KeyVibe-006 — Vibostolimab + pembrolizumab vs durvalumab
deck: Backtested directional hit. The framework would have called KeyVibe-006 a FAIL before readout on structural grounds. Dec 16, 2024: Discontinued for futility (Merck, Dec 16, 2024).
indication: Stage III unresectable NSCLC, post-cCRT consolidation
prediction_headline: Eighth Phase 3 TIGIT + PD-1 test. Every prior Phase 3 TIGIT trial had failed across four cancer types. The framework places KeyVibe-006 in the same structural class as the seven that had already missed.
result_note: Readout source: Merck press release Dec 16, 2024. Full data pending publication.

title: LATIFY — Ceralasertib (ATR) + durvalumab vs docetaxel
deck: Backtested directional hit. The framework would have called LATIFY a FAIL before readout on structural grounds. Dec 22, 2025: Missed primary OS endpoint (AstraZeneca, Dec 22, 2025).
prediction_headline: ATR inhibition adds a DNA-damage-response layer in a disease where the tumour has already escaped immune surveillance. The combination does not address the structural reason first-line ICI failed.
result_note: Readout source: AstraZeneca pipeline update Dec 22, 2025.

title: TiNivo-2 — Tivozanib + nivolumab vs tivozanib
deck: Backtested directional hit. The framework would have called TiNivo-2 a FAIL before readout on structural grounds. Sep 15, 2024: Missed primary PFS endpoint (ESMO 2024).
prediction_headline: Continuing PD-1 blockade after prior IO failure adds nothing structural over the TKI alone. The tumour has already escaped the immune step the combination is trying to re-engage.
result_note: Readout source: ESMO 2024 presentation / AVEO July 18 2024 release.

title: DeLLphi-312 — Tarlatamab + durvalumab + chemo vs durvalumab + chemo
deck: Prospective prediction. Framework calls this equivalent to the durvalumab + chemo backbone on ORR. Filed before readout.
drug: Tarlatamab + durvalumab + chemo vs durvalumab + chemo
prediction_headline: Adding tarlatamab to a durvalumab + platinum/etoposide backbone in 1L ES-SCLC is a minor structural increment in an already-saturated first-line window. Framework predicts equivalent ORR, delta within ±5 points.
result_note: Pipeline-generated prediction. Status: active. Falsifier: OS_HR delta outside [-5, +5] refutes the EQUIVALENT call.
